headline: Surgeons performed the world's first whole-eye transplant
meta_description: NYU surgeons completed the first whole-eye transplant in history. The eye remained healthy — a foundation for restoring vision.
summary_short: A team in New York transplanted an entire human eye for the first time — and it stayed healthy, opening a path toward restoring sight.
body:
For centuries, transplanting a whole human eye was considered impossible — the organ is too delicate, its connection to the brain too complex. Then a surgical team at NYU Langone Health did it, transplanting an entire eye as part of a face transplant for a power-line worker who survived a severe electrical accident.

Well over a year on, the transplanted eye has remained healthy, with blood flow to the retina — something many experts doubted could be achieved. The patient's sight has not returned yet, but the door is open: medicine finally has living proof that the human eye can survive transplantation, the essential first step toward one day restoring vision to the blind.
